summary: Generated a review-ready five-file web-capture example package from the scaffold and ran a privacy/provider-neutrality scan; the final scan reported 5 Markdown files and 0 issues.
next_angle: Run the formal verify phase against the generated package: confirm goal alignment, source coverage, explicit constraints, evidence risks, contradictions, and readiness to move to finalization.
open_questions: Formal adequacy verification still needs to confirm that the generated package is sufficient for finalization.

sources.title: RFC 9309: Robots Exclusion Protocol
sources.note: Supports the package distinction between crawler politeness and authorization.

sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
sources.note: Supports the safe-method, redirect, and HTTP provenance guidance.

sources.title: OWASP Server-Side Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Supports URL-controlled fetch containment, allowlists, parser abuse, and network controls.

sources.title: OWASP LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Supports treating captured pages and Markdown as untrusted evidence.

sources.title: CommonMark Spec 0.31.2
sources.note: Supports the warning that Markdown can include raw HTML constructs and should not be treated as sanitized output.

findings.text: A candidate public example package now exists with README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md under workspace/outputs/web-capture-evaluation/.
The generated files explicitly frame captured pages and converted Markdown as untrusted evidence, keep Markdown conversion separate from sanitization, and keep the workflow converter-neutral.
The first scan caught two overly literal phrases matching raw-transcript/internal-dump markers; the package text was revised and the second scan reported zero issues.
The next worker should run a formal verify phase before finalization, using the generated package and scan report as primary artifacts.

analysis_artifacts.note: Generated the five Markdown package files and performed the constraint scan.
Machine-readable scan report showing the generated file list and zero remaining privacy/provider-neutrality issues.

adequacy.goal_alignment: A candidate package has been generated for the requested public-safe repository example, but the dedicated verify phase has not yet judged adequacy.
adequacy.coverage_summary: The package covers the requested five files, English public-repo wording, provider-neutral workflow, primary-source anchors, and a clean privacy/local-marker scan. Formal adequacy verification remains open.

covered_requirements.requirement: Generate README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md.
covered_requirements.evidence: workspace/outputs/web-capture-evaluation contains all five requested Markdown files.

covered_requirements.requirement: Keep user-facing materials in English and provider-neutral.
covered_requirements.evidence: Generated package text is English and describes converter-neutral workflow boundaries.

covered_requirements.requirement: Avoid private paths, chat identifiers, tokens, local-only tool names, and unsanitized transcript content.

coverage_gaps.gap: A formal adequacy review has not yet been run in phase=verify.

evidence_risks.risk: The package is a synthesized example from prior evidence and intentionally does not include a live capture fixture.

adequacy.recommended_next_angle: Evaluate whether the generated package is sufficient for the goal and constraints; if passed, route to finalization.
blocking_reasons: Formal adequacy verification is still required before finalization.

validation_evidence.reason: The second scan of the generated package found zero private paths, chat identifiers, local-only tool names, or raw transcript/internal-dump markers.
